lines.x: Ursus, Icefield entrance, Wright-Kochinski Scientific Investigation Supply Depot
Phew— Finally here.
*beep* *beep* Please enter passphrase.
*ahem*—Columbian Billy Bro, Ursus Cautus Dossoles, Horosho!

Verifying passphrase...
Confirmed. Xорошо, Columbian.
Verifying speaker...
Confirmed.
Rhine Lab, serial number CRL005—
(Strange tone) My-ma-meh-ma-ah~gah~lan~
Welcome to the Wright-Kochinski Scientific Investigation Supply Depot.
Hmm—
(Fishes out notebook) 'Audio system in Ursus W-K station is slightly damaged—'
'—Maintenance unnecessary for now.'
That should do.
Professor Mary's team probably took enough supplies for, let's see, one, two, three... twenty people?!
Twenty people's worth of supplies for a team of fifteen...
(Fishes out notebook) 'Only five percent of supplies remaining, enough for ten people (excluding myself)—requires restocking.'
Now what else—
Structure... shows no signs of damage. Energy... no recharge needed. Life support equipment... still good, temperature's alright.
First aid kits appear to be unused, doors and windows show no sign of external damage...
And finally...
(Operates supply depot equipment)
Yup, that's the route.
(Confirms signals returning from outposts along the route)
The routine reports along the way suggest they didn't run into any trouble.
Two months ago
Columbia, Trimounts, Rhine Lab Headquarters, Scientific Investigation Section
Alright, I'll prepare to depart then. Anything else you need me for?
That should be all.
(Quietly) There's one last thing, but you can't tell anyone.
Huh? Sure, I can keep a secret.
(Glances around)
(Quietly) Director Maryam's team went missing in the icefield.
Huh?! What happened to Professor Mary—
Wmmmph!!
(Quietly) Keep it down!
Mmf, mrrmrmff.
(Quietly) Anyway, that's the gist of it.
(Quietly) So your top priority on this trip is to find and rescue the Director's team.
(Quietly) The Exploration Society's already organizing their own search and rescue, but I'm sure you know how inefficient they always are.
(Quietly) So... you're the only one they can count on.
(Quietly) Got it.
(Operates supply depot equipment)
The other outposts seem normal.
Which means they're still together.
That, or...
Hmm?
A signal from a Columbian outpost?
(Operates supply depot equipment)
(Picks up receiver)
This is @#￥-bian base—
We're under￥!@%&! Need... #￥!
Coordinates—
A signal sent by Professor Mary himself... Which means he should still be there!
But it would take some time for the signal to travel all the way here...
(Pats her own cheeks)
First things first, Magallan.
(Presses console button)
*Beep beep*
CRL005, Magallan.
Identity verified. How may I help you?
Give me both the outgoing and incoming signal logs from Columbia in the last three or so months, thanks.
Confirmed. Beginning encrypted callbacks to Messenger Outpost.
(Switches console off)
Ursus uses the base station established by the Exploration Society too, so that'll be convenient for applying for an icefield entry permit...
I can go as soon as the Messenger from Ursus gets here to confirm the documents.
I'm on my way, Professor, so stay out of any serious trouble.
That was fast— Please give me a moment, I'll be right there!
Hi there.
Miss Venta Magallan.
Is this Mr. Ivan?
Yes, on business as usual.
One sec, I'll open the door for you.
Hello, Mr. Ivan. Would you like to take a rest inside?
You know the rules, Miss Magallan. If I take even half a step forward, we'll never hear the end of it from the Columbians.
It's just us here. Who's gonna know?
Your little machine is already keeping a close eye on us.
Between you and me, the systems here aren't as smart as you'd think, haha.
Still, better to be safe than sorry when dealing with the Columbians.
Not that I don't trust you, of course. But allow me to decline your kind invitation out of an abundance of caution.
Now then, if you could show me your documents?
Hyup.
That should be all of them.
Hm... entry permit... expedition permit... expedition route confirmation... cartography commission...
Very good, Miss Magallan, everything seems to be in order.
Phew—
That said, I have some unfortunate news for you—
All icefield expedition permits this year have been cancelled.
Wha?!
That's, but... the documents should all be...
I doubt this is the first time you've experienced this, so please try to understand our situation.
But I have to go, it's urgent. Could... Could you please manage some kind of workaround?
It's not impossible, if you can tell me why.
Really?!
Really.
So the thing is, we have a missing team out there and I need to find them, or at least confirm their situation.
There are lives at stake here, so whatever conditions there are, I'll agree to them after I find them.
Then you should have reported this through official channels and waited for an Ursus rescue team. Why set out on your own?
I understand, I really do, but an official rescue team needs time to prepare, right?
I'm an explorer too—one pretty familiar with the icefield if I do say so myself—and finding them even a day earlier will make the follow-up rescue that much simpler.
Also...
I understand.
Then do you mind sharing the missing group's last known coordinates?
Eh? Oh, okay, just give me a moment.
They should be around here.
Thank you.
(Ursine dialect) I've obtained the Columbians' coordinates. Based on them, they likely have something to do with what's happening in the north.
(Ursine dialect) Very good.
(Ursine dialect) What do we do with the girl?
(Ursine dialect) She's supposedly a veteran explorer from Columbia, but her importance to the country is unclear. Keep an eye on her and await further instructions.
(Ursine dialect) Understood.
Mr. Ivan...
I just confirmed with my colleagues. My apologies, Miss Magallan, but we still can't let you head any further north.
The front is under military control, so it's out of my hands.
Then...
But to make up for it, I can accompany you anywhere within Ursus territory. My status as an Ursus official should simplify things for you.
Let me think then...
Oh right, Sami!
No one would block me if I entered the icefield through Sami, right?
In theory, yes. However—
Then let's go to Sami first!
I'm all ready, Mr. Ivan. Please take me there now.
(Ursine dialect) She wants to enter the icefield through Sami. Do you have enough time to run a background check?
(Ursine dialect) Just do what she wants. Investigating a Columbian with a public identity won't take long.
(Ursine dialect) When you reach the border, the guards will send someone to take over your job.
(Ursine dialect) Roger.
(Ursine dialect) Be careful not to let the Witch spot you.
(Ursine dialect) Understood.
As you wish, Miss Magallan.
Captain, a moment please.
You have a question, soldier?
Yes, Captain. Regarding... our mission.
You have concerns about the military's arrangements?
No, sir. I wouldn't dare. I was only thinking, about how we treat the Sami—
You pity those creatures?
Yes, Captain. I think—
Wait, what did you just say?
I can understand your sympathy.
Let me ask you a question though, soldier.
As I recall, you're the son of a farmer.
Yes, Captain.
Then, on your family's land, you must breed burdenbeasts. You use them to pull your carts, and otherwise create value as your father demands.
Do you think those burdenbeasts would have done any of that on their own, without your father to tame them?
Of course not, Captain.
So what makes you think the Sami would?
But, Captain—
After the Ursus people overthrew the Hippogryph's rule, they spent a millennium transforming the land into what Ursus needed it to be.
And yet Sami remains a desolate woodland.
The Sami have contributed nothing to civilization.
I believe that with proper guidance, they will eventually come to understand the painstaking efforts Ursus has made here.
We've brought them the fire of civilization, so they should be shedding tears of gratitude.
Yet their response, well, I believe it goes without saying.
Yes, Captain.
They understand only strength, and more civilized means of persuasion fall on deaf ears.
As such, violence is a necessary evil.
But when their descendants are living in sturdy homes and enjoying the products of Ursus culture...
—Then they'll understand.
Understood, Captain.
Reporting in. A group of Sami sighted ahead, engaged in combat with the demons.
Get in formation, and have the Caster team ready their Arts.
Yes, sir!
Captain, you...
Not even a disobedient burdenbeast deserves to be devoured by starving beasts, wouldn't you agree?
All units, prepare to attack the demons.
—Yes, sir!
One week later, Sami-Ursus border
Just a little further, and we'll reach the border sentry outpost. And Sami is a short trek from there.
This is as far as I can accompany you.
It's been a whole lot easier communicating with anyone from Ursus with you around.
Your Ursine isn't bad, although your accent is thick.
I doubt I'll be fixing that anytime soon, heh.
And I still have a lot to learn. Sometimes I can't understand a word you're saying, Mr. Ivan.
It's an obscure regional dialect. Not even other Ursus can understand me sometimes.
I could teach you some on your next visit, if you're not so busy next time.
Really? That'd be wonderful, thanks!
Ha, you're very welcome.
By the way, are you particularly familiar with Sami?
Well, I've never used this route before, so not really.

But I've heard it takes just as many supplies to get through Sami as it does to enter the icefield, and there are fewer outposts there as you get nearer to the icefield, so restocking is difficult too.
Indeed, it is a savage place.
You don't seem to like it much, Mr. Ivan.
Normally, there is a permanent garrison at every outpost on the Ursus border, but the Sami-Ursus border only has regular patrols. Can you guess why?
Does... Ursus have some sort of dispute with Sami?
Dispute? The Sami invaded Ursus territory and attacked our soldiers, all to satisfy their malicious, primitive desires.
Even the sentry outpost up ahead was once occupied by the Sami for no reason at all. We even so kindly helped them repel the demons before issuing a protest to reclaim our territory.
But they refused to leave, and even harmed our soldiers. In the end we had no choice but to drive them out with force.
Is that so...
(Is that so...?)
The barbarous Sami exacted their revenge by sending out a vengeful spirit we call the Witch.
She killed every last soldier at the outpost, and has been wandering the border ever since.
Whenever she runs across a small squad, she'll engulf them in a snowstorm before going in for the kill, but if she meets a larger force she'll hide in the wind and snow.
We can neither get rid of her nor chase her away. A real thorn in our side.
In the end, our only option is to strengthen the border defenses with an elite patrol squad, simply to prevent the Sami from wreaking further havoc.
You must also be careful when passing through Sami.
The moment they spot an outsider, they will not stop until they rip out your throat to sip on your blood.
Like the wild beasts swarming the snowfield, we can only keep them in check by force.
Am I clear?
Y-Yeah, you're clear.
Good.
It's windy out here. Let's hurry to the outpost and get some rest.
Is the monster still there?!
It's been driven off, so we should be safe for now.
Phew... wonderful.
Hey, friends from Ursus! How is the situation over there?!
No casualties on our end.
Freya, do you still have any extra water? Bring some for our Ursus friends.
At once.
Phew. That was a little dangerous earlier. If you guys haven't helped out, some of our fledglings here might already be on their way to meet the ancestors.
Please, there's no need for that. We share a common enemy.
Where are you people headed to next?
We just happened to be passing by while performing our military duties.
The people of Ursus sure are stiff. We just fought demons, and you're still thinking about these 'military duties' of yours. Your brothers must be exhausted.
Come rest at our camp. Warm yourselves by the fire, and have some dried meat before hitting the road again, friend.
Then... I suppose it would be rude to refuse.
Hahahaha. There's no need to be so polite.
Freya, where's that water?!
I'm still patching someone up, give me a moment!
Forget about it then, we're bringing them back to camp for drinks!
Santalla!
What wrong?!
Double-check that there are no more demons in the area, while I take the wounded and our Ursus friends back to camp!
Got it, then I'll return when I'm done. Remember to leave me a spot under the tree!
But of course.
Get up, kiddos! Time to head home!
Listen up, soldiers.
Advance in formation.
Is this really a sentry outpost? It's so spacious!
And this tree smack dab in the center. Does Ursus have a custom of planting trees in the middle of buildings, Mr. Ivan?
It was planted by the Sami, and has been dead for a long time.
But fortunately it's sturdy enough to act as a much needed barrier out here in the snowfield. So we decided to let it stand rather than cutting it down for firewood.
There aren't any other trees in a hundred miles. So why would they randomly plant one right in the middle of the snowfield?
I wouldn't know.
Can I... collect some samples?
By all means.
(Ursine dialect) We've arrived at the dead tree outpost; the Witch has not bothered us.
(Ursine dialect) We've finished our investigation into the girl, too. Her background is unremarkable, but we can make use of her expertise.
(Ursine dialect) The border guards will move to your position as soon as they can. They'll take the girl and pass her on to the investigation team, who need someone to guide them across the icefield.
(Ursine dialect) Your role ends there.
(Ursine dialect) Understood. I'll go ahead and prepare the signal beacon to connect at any time.
(Ursine dialect) My apologies, little one.
My apologies, 'friend.'
What are you doing?! Lower your weapons!
My duty is to expand our territory for His Majesty the Emperor.
What?
From today onwards, this too is Ursus territory.
No, what? What exactly do you mean?!
Did I not make myself clear, barbarian?
This is the only settlement within a hundred kilometers. Once we occupy it, great Ursus will naturally be the master of this land.
As this is now Ursus territory, I must request that you leave.
We've been living here for ages, while all you've done is plant a flag! What grounds do you have to claim this land as yours?!
Because we're here now. No more reason is needed.
Hmm... In honor of our friendship and mutual assistance against the demons, I'll give each of you two choices. Leave this place at once, or become subjects of Ursus.
You and your people are excellent warriors, so our nation will gladly welcome you.
Of course, you must learn to speak Ursine, wear Ursus clothing, and most importantly, abandon your abominable and heretical beliefs.
You!
(Silently mouths a spell)
Caster, shut that witch up.
Urgh...
Freya!
Don't do anything foolish, and don't even think about resisting.
(Speaking in Sami) O old tree, I plead you bear witness in my stead...
HEAAAAARGH!!!!!
You leave me no choice, 'friend.'
All units, attack.
(Ursine dialect) Please speak.

(Ursine dialect) The wind from the direction of the border guards seems off, so there might be an incoming snowstorm. Find an open space to deploy a signal beacon for them, and upon making contact, take the target and leave the area immediately.
(Ursine dialect) Understood.
I see you're done collecting samples.
This tree has some amazing properties. Biologically speaking, it's completely withered. But what's strange is how parts of it still feel alive and well.
With more thorough research, I might even be able to publish a whole paper on it.
A great blessing to academia, no doubt.
But there's a snowstorm coming, so you'd better go shelter in the outpost first.
Ah, gotcha.
You're not joining me, Mr. Ivan?
I'm going to go check the scale of the storm, so that if the weather worsens we'll still have a chance to send for help.
Oh, okay.
Please, have some of this before you go.
I just heated up some water, so have a cup to warm yourself up.
That's alright, I'll be right back.
Mr. Ivan...
Wait, why am I hiding in here by the fire while he's hard at work outside? That doesn't seem right...
I'm an explorer, aren't I? How can I let my companion face this alone?
Maybe I should—
So cold...
(Looks towards window)
Oww, my face hurts...
The wind's picking up.
You need to be careful, Mr. Ivan.
That's...
Mr. Ivan?
No, that's not, that's—?!
A gust of wind kicks up waves of snow, pouring towards the outpost.
However, the waves seem to advance only sluggishly, at a controlled speed. As if commanded to do so.
This is not an optical illusion caused by the distance, but the wind and snow are indeed following orders.
They dare not let even a snowflake overtake their leader without permission.
And she, currently leading the wind's charge, holds her scepter high as she slowly makes her way towards the outpost.
One step, then another.
Smooth, steady, unquestionable.
Until the land is buried beneath the wind and snow.
Or so it might have gone. But Magallan welcomes this scene, more than anyone else on the northern frontier.
Because the woman walking amongst the ice and snow is her best friend on the icefield.
Simone!
As her shout travels across the snow, Magallan clearly feels the wind subside.
The waves of snow behind Simone vanish without a trace as she walks up to Magallan.
Hello, Magallan.
Shouldn't you be out on an icefield expedition? What brings you here?
A professor I respect has gone missing out there, but Ursus blocked the route, again. So I have to take the long way around.
I warmed up some tea, want some?
Why, thank you.
*glug*...*glug*...
Phew—
So, you want to enter the icefield from Sami.
Uh-huh.
Ah, right. Simone, you're a Sami, right?
That I am.
Then you—
How could I possibly not help out a good acquaintance?
Ehehe~
Okay, then I'll say goodbye to Mr. Ivan when he gets back, and then we can head into Sami.
Although I've heard it's kinda primitive there, and really scary.
Is that... what the Ursus Messenger told you?
He also said there's a witch around here who attacks people for no reason, so I should be careful.
The Ursus are all like that.
So what kind of person do you imagine this witch to be?
Hmm... a little scary, just like this tree.
*sigh* Mr. Ivan really seems to loathe Sami, though.
He was so upfront about it, too.
Us Sami hate the Ursus just as much.
But why? Couldn't you just talk out this misunderstanding?
Because to them, conquest is far simpler than understanding. They may see just one annoying witch, but countless Sami have already—
—No, it's getting late. Where did you say your friend was headed?
North, towards the snowstorm.
Wait here, I'll go help him out.
I think it's better if we go together—
Stay put, Magallan.
You're safer in here.
O-Okay then...
Be careful out there, Simone.
Mhm.
Um, and be careful of the witch, yeah?
Hah. If she does show up, I'll be sure to stay far away from her.
In the meantime, could you warm up some more tea?
Mmhmm!
And now I'm all alone, again.
I hope Mr. Ivan and Simone can get along out there...
*sigh*...
What do you think, Mr. Tree?
The ancient, dead tree responds with silence.
I still don't get it. How can they fight off a common enemy together one minute, and go for each other's throats the next?
Isn't cooperation obviously the better choice?
The venerable tree ponders for a moment, and decides to answer by smacking its withered branches together.
What about you, Mr. Tree? Did you come here to this snowfield all on your own because other people couldn't understand you?
The towering tree feels the question is difficult to answer, so chooses to remain silent.
You're right... that was a stupid question.
But if possible, I still hope we can—
I'm back.
Simone?
Oh, here's your tea.
Thank you.
So where's Mr. Ivan?
I looked where you told me to, but couldn't find the Messenger you mentioned.
The storm from the north is close.
Perhaps he's already rendezvoused with the border guards he called for help, and taken refuge with them.
Ursus sentry outposts aren't sturdy enough to withstand a snowstorm this strong. This building will be completely crushed.
We might also want to...
Can we... wait just a bit longer? Mr. Ivan is a pretty experienced Messenger, and we've known each other a long time, so I don't think he'd be caught by a snowstorm like this.
If you insist. Just a bit longer.
I'll let the old tree know that we're safe.
The old tree... Simone, are you from around here?
It used to be my duty to protect this unchanging old tree.
It may have transcended death, but failed to be remembered.
And to think all this happened because of a moment of kind-heartedness.
We sow goodwill only to reap evil. This is how the land does things.
So, that's why there's a... witch roaming nearby.
Perhaps.
It's about time. We'll be trapped in here if we don't get moving.
What are you doing?
Finding a spot protected from the wind, to leave a mark for him.
I have to at least let him know I made it out safely.
Hmm... that should do it.
I'm leaving, Mr. Ivan.
Goodbye.
Let's go, Magallan.
Mhm.
The wind and snow seem so docile when I'm with you, Simone.
Just a little Arts I happen to know.
Hmm... That communicator on your shoulder... I feel like I've seen it somewhere before.
This? I bought it from the Ursus. It works quite well.
Do you need one?
I'm good. Columbian communicators are way easier to use than Ursus ones.
Ah... Simone?
Yes?
There's something... I need to apologize to you for. I hope you won't get angry.
I won't know until I hear it.
Do we have news on Lazal and the other men who disappeared?
Yeah, we can stop searching.
Demons have been through here.
The ice there has Arts damage. It was a hard fight.
They are all roots of the Frost Maple and respected warriors, but not even their bodies remained?
Bad weather is known to befall the icefield.
But this kind of weather has been happening far too often of late.
Hadn't Sami long since warned us through Eikthyrnir?
Come, we must reinforce the fortifications and keep a close eye on every mountain pass.
(Logs in notebook) 'We've entered the Fjal Vetrtonn. The current temperature is... 31 degrees below zero.'
'So far, I have yet to find any comms stations within Sami borders... and haven't been able to make contact with Director Maryam's expedition team.'
*sigh*... If Professor Mary isn't around, who am I even going to send these notes to? Every time he reads my reports, he points out all the phenomena I should've paid closer attention to...
'We've passed through the local settlement mentioned in previous expedition records: at the foot of the Fjal Vetrtonn, near the edge of the forest.'
*huff*... Even though I only lost a single glove, it feels so cold.
Need a hand, Magallan?
Oh, it's alright, Simone. I'm almost done with this report! I'll be ready to leave the shelter and brave the blizzard soon!
Now where was I? Right...
'The settlement had vanished.'
'I asked Simone—'
(Quickly erases)
'I asked an accompanying Sami about this, and it seems that Sami tribes are known to move around.'
Why did they move?
I tried looking into it, but couldn't figure anything out.
Simone told me that it was Sami herself. She told them that danger was drawing near and that they had to leave.

But the climate data we've collected so far aren't all that different from the historical record, and there don't seem to be any changes in long term trends either. So what kind of danger is Sami facing?
Looks like... the Scientific Investigation Section's data... needs to be updated.
Right, Sami is still a blind spot in our knowledge.
But it seems the Sami really don't like being disturbed.
Professor Mary, you've always wanted to work with them, but haven't been able to.

It takes so much effort to get our applications processed in Ursus. If we could just find a new path into the icefield through Sami and build more research stations, our expeditions would be so much easier.
Unfortunately... the Sami don't want anyone in the icefield...
Right...
Eh?
Was I just... talking to Professor Mary?
Simone. Simone!
What's the matter?
I just—
—Do you see a group of people over there? There's too much snow; I can't quite...
Mag...allan...
I-Is it really Professor Mary?!
Simone, I need to head over there for a look!
Magallan.
(Ah! It's really you! Thank goodness, I found you guys! I knew you'd be safe!)
(Weird... How come I can't speak?)
(*huff*... *huff*... My heart's beating too fast... A-Am I trembling? Why?)
(No, no, no! I need to stop the Professor and take them back!)
Professor—
—Mmf, mrrmrmff!
Shh.
Don't listen. Don't look. Don't speak.
I don't blame you, Simone. It's been a long time since your last visit. It's no wonder you didn't sense it.
Haha... I see, so now I'm a visitor here?
Not as much as her, but still a visitor. The route you two took hasn't been used for a long time.
*sigh* You're lucky I take this detour every time.
So the warriors still haven't accepted you, even now? But the land herself clearly regards you as a beloved child.
...In any case, thank you for your help, Typhon.
Mhm. No need to pay me back.
The food I hid around here is more than enough to last me a few months.
So you said you were heading to the icefield to look for missing southerners?
Yes.
Then let me come with you. The missing men must have run into trouble. I'll help you protect them.
Uh—Um—Eh, I can talk now...? Is it okay if I talk?
Listen—
Don't move.
...Why are you raising your hands?
Uh... Because I heard the Sami are very fierce? And they don't like it when others enter the icefield?
I-I'm really not plotting anything bad!
Hm? Well, the warriors here are indeed fierce, but I'm no warrior.
Anyway, I woke you up just now with my Arts, but you can still hear the voices, right?
Mm... Yeah, I can.
It's okay. I've realized it's a hallucination by now. I'm not stupid enough to keep talking to it.
Right, I should jot this down in my notes, too.
So do you know what causes these hallucinations? Exhaustion due to the extreme weather? Maybe something in the water that paralyzes the central nervous system?
*sigh*... Come here. Your ear.
(Whispers) Okay, go ahead and tell me.
Alright, now the other side.
Okay... Huh? Weren't you going to whisper something?
Did you just rub something on my ears? It felt kinda cold.
Now put this under your tongue and press down on it.
M-Moss and a ball of mud?
I know that explorers sometimes boil leather when they're completely out of food, but I've never considered eating moss in an emergency...
Alright, Magallan, be brave and give it a shot!
Stop being so suspicious of everything. Just put it in your mouth.
Now you'll only hear your own voice and won't be bothered by those strange noises.
Really? You're so helpful!
...Wait, but won't I be deaf to any environmental dangers, too? It'll also affect my balance when I climb. It's too risky.
Don't be afraid. Just trust Typhon.
We'll take you somewhere where the snow's not contaminated.
Yeah. We'll give you a hand signal when you can spit the mud out.
Until then, don't talk too loud. You don't want it to hear you.
Hmm, alright...
The young explorer suddenly stops and looks in a direction where no one's standing.
The brief pause lasts only two seconds. She quickly follows Typhon's directions and squeezes the mudball into her mouth, as though nothing unusual had happened.
Yeah, I can't hear anything now.
Stop.
Ehh?
There's musbeast fur scattered in the snow. It's a kind of detection Arts.
Good thing you held Magallan back.
We couldn't escape the warriors' eyes, but at the very least our visit seems a little more respectful this way.
Should we have her spit out the mudball? You can fill her in on the situation.
If her plan was to enter the icefield this way, then she probably knows nothing about the northern front.
No need. Leave the negotiations to me. Most of these warriors would chase us off with their hammers the moment they hear Columbian or the common tongue.
Ah... see?
*sigh* It's the Legion. They aren't going to talk to us.
You, daughter of the Santalla Tree.
Greetings, warriors of Treescar.
You're with an outlander.
And... the hunter with the black bow.
Indeed.
But please don't be so quick to attack. This outlander asks nothing of Sami.
We simply seek to enter the endless icefield.
The Sami warriors surround them in silence. They show no intention of backing off.
Though silent, their stances remain unchanged, with their hands on their weapons. Regardless, Santalla seems to know exactly what it is they need to hear.

...I understand. It's against the rules. No one should enter the icefield rashly. You built these high walls not only to keep the icefield's disasters at bay, but also to stop the Sami and wild beasts.
But her dear mentor has disappeared in the icefield. She is earnestly trying to rescue someone who is practically family to her, and I hope you can make an exception.
The Sami warriors do not move an inch.
...Please trust us. We will take care of the southerner and prevent her from acting rashly against Sami. She won't become lost, and absolutely won't become your enemy.
We are accompanying her in order to take care of any worst case scenarios that might happen to her or the missing men.
...Trust that black bow, too?
You want us to trust the daughter of a crazed Cyclops?
That black bow means nothing. She fights the same enemy as you.
Her mind may be clear, but that doesn't mean her weapon won't bring misfortune.
There was a time when we brought back the belongings of every last fallen warrior, until we realized that the enemy could find us through their contaminated relics.
I'm sure you know this well.
—Simone from under the Santalla Tree.
I am a shaman no longer, but if that identity will make you reconsider, then I am more than happy to use it.
There are still many among us who remember the warrior who almost became a Snowpriest. But she ran from her duties, so we do not intend to bring up her past.
Her power can still take care of those around her.
The team that went missing amounts to more than a dozen men. The northern warriors I know would never turn a blind eye with so many lives on the line.
No.
You must bear the harshness of fate, daughter of the Santalla Tree. Endure it. Do not run away.
...Those are Eikthyrnir's words.
Did he... learn something from his divination? About the whole northern realm?
I have no answers for outsiders.
But Eikthyrnir has already led many a warrior south, to inform each tribe of Sami's whispers.
I understand.
If Sami has made her prophecy, I shan't insist on entering the icefield.
But now that it's come to this, we lack the supplies to head back down the mountain and take the long way around.
Even though the demons' contamination spreads without end, we have no choice but to continue our journey through the mountains, and through the pass guarded by your warriors.
Supplies? You only have the southerners' shortcomings to blame. They claim the land a barren wilderness, which is why they find themselves unable to survive in nature.
I'm simply asking that you—
*sigh* Let's go, Simone.
You don't owe them an explanation.
Wait, that hand signal... Are you for real?
Yeah, trust me.
—Just hold on a moment!
Ugh... So bitter... *cough*... Let me explain!
I'm not a bad guy, I'm just an explorer! The only thing in these crates are reserve drones. I can open them up if you want a look!
I'm not trying to sneak into the icefield, either. I just didn't know how to get in touch with the Sami government to submit an application!
Er, wait, does Sami have a government? I haven't actually seen anything resembling one, but...
But I do have the documents I was going to hand over to Ursus. My ID, exploration permit, and the abandoned route plans... They all prove that I'm just an explorer, nothing more.
Anyway, Simone and Typhon have both been so nice to me. Please don't blame them! If it's me that you're suspicious of, I'll explain everything!
Also...
Huh?
She's not imagining things.
Everyone's expressions grow increasingly solemn with every word she says.
They're all looking behind her, as though they too can hear her hallucinations.
Run, you birdbrain!
You brought an omen of disaster right into the group!
This way!
Is this the path you mentioned?
That's right.
Ah—That was close! The arrow hit the rock!
Thanks, rock! Thanks, Fjal Vetrtonn!
They probably mistook it for one of us, in this thick snowfall.
Your Arts come in handy, Simone. I knew I could trust you to keep us hidden.
Okay, now this way.
It's gotten quieter.
Mm... Let me check. Yeah, I don't see them anymore.
Good! We shook them off!
Or perhaps... they stopped giving chase.
Whatever the case, we should be safe now, right?
Phew... Hah...
All of a sudden... I'm outta breath...
Give me your hand. I'll help you.

Th-Thanks... The expedition equipment and my protective gear are all just too heavy. When they designed this extreme north exploration gear, they probably didn't think we'd need to run in the mountains...
No, wait... I need to apologize!
I couldn't hear anything earlier, but it looked like you were bickering because of me. And then I got ahead of myself and started shouting.
The Director always tells me to think before I act, but... Maybe it's because I have no idea what's so scary about these hallucinations, but I didn't really think that through, did I?
No need to apologize.
Nature doesn't debate right and wrong. All that matters is closeness. The same goes for those northern warriors just now.
Uh... closeness...?
They're just like, mm... like a chain of mountains. No matter what you say to them, you will never become part of the mountains. All you can do is try your hardest to surmount them.
Of course, you also can't tell the mountains that they're wrong and should be flat ground instead.
Ehh?
Why are they so unwilling to talk it out?
Let's set aside whether they could even understand you. Those warriors normally have no need for words.
See, if you and I lived together all day, every day, and everything we did was the same daily routine: patrolling, hunting, fighting... We wouldn't have much reason to talk.
Hm, because we'd be too used to everything?
No. Because communication is only needed when we think different thoughts.
That's why I called them a chain of mountains.
Besides, they have to deal with the Ursus, too.
To them, they must be wary of any outlanders whose intentions they cannot discern at a single glance.
It may seem a little cruel, but it's how they have to live... (In Sami) 'One must accept fate, no matter what form it takes.'
(In Sami) Fate... hm.

What's done is done. The signal I gave back there was for Simone to run. When you're dealing with those warriors, most of the time you're going to have to back down. You can't count on them giving way.
That said, now we can't reach the icefield to find your teacher.
*sigh* Yeah...
So what are you going to do now?
I guess... I'll go to Čappat to request help.
That's the southernmost city in Sami, isn't it?
Yeah, it's where a lot of Columbian companies have their only branch offices in Sami.
Now that the Ursus route is sealed off, it's the only place the Exploration Society can mount a rescue from.
I'll rendezvous with them and try my best to collect information on the route. After all, nobody's really all that familiar with Sami.
I'll keep escorting you, then.
I don't need payment. I have no use for most of the trinkets southerners offer. I just happen to have a client waiting for me in Čappat.
Whoa, thanks!
What about you, Simone?
I promised I'd stay with you, didn't I?
Ehehe.
But Typhon, where does this path lead?
I find it hard to imagine that there are still paths in this mountain that the scouts haven't discovered.
That's because this takes us to where Arges lives.
...A Cyclops's cave.
Yeah, I guess it's my home, too. At least, I grew up there.
I may have used Sarkaz Arts to cover our tracks, but any veteran shaman is bound to figure it out in an instant. But I doubt they'd want to disturb a Cyclops.
Heh, no one likes hearing the prophesized tragedies doomed to befall them, after all.
If they'd kept chase, I'd have to count on Arges being home to help us out. Luckily they weren't so patient.
This is making me feel uneasy, though.
They gave up far too easily. Almost as if they abandoned us in the snow.
There's nothing to fear; I'm watching our surroundings.
The snow creaks beneath their feet.
Oh right... The disaster you two mentioned earlier... What's that all about?
If we're able to find the source of the auditory hallucinations and bring back samples to Rhine for analysis, maybe we could find a more long-term remedy!
Neither of them answer Magallan right away. Instead, the sound of their boots sinking into the snow grows louder by the step.
What a strange question. Do you people think that Catastrophes, the cold, or the fangs of a mangler will just up and disappear one day?
The disaster is relatively less common, but if it comes knocking on your door, you cannot defeat it.
Hm, just like now.
The sound of their footsteps in the snow stops. While she wasn't paying attention, the snow had become light as a feather. Magallan blinks and does a double take.
A-Am I hallucinating again, or is...
...is it snowing upwards?
No, your eyes aren't deceiving you.
The snow is floating midair, above our heads.
I... I need to gather samples!
Give me a minute to activate the drones!
A snowflake that's still floating upward...
Hah, got it! Next, snow that's accumulated midair...
—Watch out!
The snow crumbles downward.
And from what I can barely make out given the visibility, there seems to be a lot more accumulated in the sky up ahead.
If we keep going, it's enough to bury all three of us once it comes crumbling down.
This phenomenon must've been around for a while. They already knew about it, so they stopped giving chase. They knew we were prey driven into a dead end.
Not even a Snowpriest's Arts can do anything about this... and nor can I. Any snow I summon will only float upward and break free from my control.
—Wait, Magallan! Stay close to us! It's dangerous to go alone!
It's okay. I just need to get a little farther away to collect samples!
If the drones lose their balance while gathering and knock down the midair snow, only I'll be buried!
—Whoa, that was close!
Do you feel it? The snow is trembling.
Each step we take awakens it.
But a girl like her won't stop here.
Give me your hand.
Hmm?
Most people don't like doing this, but given the circumstances, we should give it a shot.
Right, to make sure the blood flows onto the arrowhead, I'll need to cut a little deeper. But you don't need me to worry about you, right?
Do what you must, Typhon. Just don't cut too deep because our hands are numbed by the cold.
The snow's coming down more quickly.
Yeah. The black seeds buried in the soil know my bow and arrows. This is exactly why people always misunderstand me.
Okay, it's just Magallan left—
—Magallan?! What's your status?
Oh, I'm fine! It's just the snow fell down and blocked the path...
Let me switch to the snow clearing module.
Okay! You two can come on over now!
Give me your hand first. Take off your glove.
Er, gimme a bit of time to steel myself first.
—Okay, here!
That'll do it.
The arrow, soaked in the three women's blood, breezes through the cold wind. With a loud bang, an entire slab of frozen snow comes crashing down onto a cliff to the side.
...Sarkaz witchcraft.
The snow up to there won't fall anymore. I'll keep an eye out for the next place that looks dangerous and deal with it ahead of time in the same way.
Let's keep going.
I found a few more dead hornbeasts close to the spring water.
We've followed the Samivilinn, the will of the land, and moved our entire tribe from the foot of the mountain all the way through the tundra.
So why aren't we free of the disaster's corrosion yet?!
We should try the divination one more time.... Just one more time, to see when the land will finally grant us peace.
...Or do you mean the Cyclops that traveled with us back then was telling the truth? That there would come a day when desolation would befall our tribe?
Didn't you say that... somebody lived here?
Yeah. And that I used to live here, too.
*sigh* She's not here, as usual.
But... Th-Th-This is a cave...
Ah, too bad Simone had to leave because something came up... If she were here, she could tell me if this is normal or not...
Hmm?
Uh, nothing! Just wondering how I should describe this place in my notes.
Whatever works for you.
Here, jerky and salt. Arges isn't stingy. You can take whatever you want.
Now we should have enough supplies to make it to the bottom of the mountain.
Thanks, Typhon, but hold on!
I don't know this Arges you're talking about. Seems a bit rude...
Hm, she herself said, and I quote: (in Sarkaz) 'Anything with its curtain pulled open can be shared.'
You can take that to mean she doesn't care.
Heh, well, don't mind if I do.
Also... is it okay if I make a detailed record of the cave in its current state?
I want to take a few pictures, too! I promise I won't make a mess!
Why even ask? When you're done, come by the fire and rest.
Gotcha!
Right, and can I look at these notes, too?
Go ahead. Only she can understand what's in there anyway.
Okay... Huh?
Right, Typhon, there's something I've been meaning to ask.
Logically speaking, when you hallucinate, you shouldn't hear any words you don't know, or any concepts you've never heard of, right?
Hm, I'm not sure I follow your 'logic.' But what about it?
Anyway, take a look at this page.
'When the box is opened, Andskotarnir will realize.'
That's the last thing I heard the Director say, before you helped me cut off the noise from my surroundings.
This Ursus communicator... It's getting a signal.
The question is... how long ago was it sent?
...Reporting... to army... border guards...
With... the help of Liebafowl... Black Mark... in Sami...
Breathe in—
Hffff—
Breath out—
Phew—
Here. Eat this.
Okay—Gah, so sour...
At least it's edible, right? You'll get used to it.
And here. This too.
Awgh—Huh? How come this nut is hollow?
Like a bubble?
Mm... yeah.
The locals call them 'bubbles.' You hardly feel anything when you eat them, but they're very important for climbing these mountains.
Do you feel better now? Does your head still hurt?
Mm—I'm fine now. These fruits and nuts are way better than altitude sickness pills, at least.
So do you know how they work?
No. But does it really matter, as long as they do?
Of course it does. I gotta jot this down and bring some samples back with me. My colleagues in pharmaceutical R&D would love to hear about this.
Did you bring any of those pills?
For altitude sickness? Of course.
You should take a look at the active ingredients.
(Rummages through baggage)
Here they are!
Main ingredients... wild sourberries and bubblenut extract... You mean...?!
One of Sami's primary exports to Columbia is raw medicinal ingredients.
Although I hear your company plans to cut off all trade with us as soon as they figure out how to synthesize or cultivate them themselves.
Unfortunately for them, that will never happen. Not with something from Sami.
You're saying we can't recreate the growth conditions in Columbia?
No, of course you can. But even if you did, what sprouts from the soil wouldn't be the same plants.
For one very simple reason.
(In Sami) Because everything is provided by Sami.
(In Sami) Everything is provided by Sami.
(In broken Sami) Everything... provided... by Sami?
You pick up languages quickly. When we first met, you needed Simone to translate for you whenever you wanted to say something about Sami.
Hehe~
Your Columbian's not bad at all either.
Arges speaks it well. That's why I can manage.
I see the tree line. The mountainfolk won't bother us anymore.
Phew, finally. A place to have a good meal.
Do trees mean better food?
The mountains have meat, but no fruit.
The forest is different. There are all kinds of food there. Anything you could want to eat.
Whoa.
(Pulls out notes and starts writing) 'Forest has abundant resources...'
Typhon, is there a settlement nearby?
Yes, and it's a tribe with a tree.
Then they must have lived here in peace for many years.
A tribe with a tree? What does that mean?
Each forest tribe has their own clan tree.
...If they don't, then they must either be up-and-coming, or they managed to lose even their tree. Whichever the case, it's laughable.
Will this tribe listen to us?
I believe they're quite friendly.
But either way, I can guarantee they will be more welcoming than the mountain tribes.
Huh? Really?
Of course. The mountain warriors fight the monsters in the north and the unfriendly Ursus all year round, so you can't blame them for being so uptight.
Some of them are less cold when they leave the frontlines for home.
Well, that's good to hear.
So, how do the Sami usually categorize themselves?

It's pretty easy to remember. The ones farthest to the north who live in the mountains are the mountainfolk, the ones who live in the middle in the forest and its surroundings are the forestfolk, and the ones who live in the wetlands to the far south are the marshfolk.
What about you, then?
Me? I'm a hunter?
A hunter? Uh, going by the classifications you just gave me, shouldn't it be based on what environment you're from?
Oh, that... I'm a Sarkaz. I don't have a fixed place to live, and I don't belong to a tribe. So naturally, I live outside the rules.
Then what about Simone?
Simone...? She—
I'm a Rhodes Islander.
Rhodes? I've never heard of an island like that here.
Is Rhodes Island another category here?
Perhaps.
Here we are.
Whoa—
This tree... It's like... a vertical nomadic city!
I-I've never seen—
—I've gotta take a picture.
It doesn't seem so different from last time, but—
Why does it look like they're all moving things outside?
(In Sami) Welcome, friends.
(In Sami) Thank you for your hospitality, Attendant of the Tree. We came from the north. Would it be convenient for us to stay the night?
(In Sami) Of course, honorable shaman. We always enjoy receiving guests. In any other circumstances, your arrival would be reason enough for a feast.
(In Sami) Alas, as you can see, we are preparing to move from this land that has long been our home. We leave on the morrow.
(In Sami) You will find many of our houses on the Tree empty tonight. You and your companions may pick any of them to rest in. The Tree won't mind.
(In Sami) I thank the Tree and your tribe for your generosity.
(In Sami) You are very welcome. Please, come in.
(In Sami) Once you have settled down, please come to the ancestral home at the top of the Tree. We may have need of your wisdom.
(Nods)
We have their permission to stay the night. Let's pick... the house closest to the branch there.
Hah... Finally, sleeping indoors.
Is that important?
How else can you rest easy? Otherwise, you're up all night from worry.
Is that so... That's awfully picky of you.
It's not. Let me explain—
Waterproof pad... sleeping bag... there!
Pft.
What's so funny?
Hm? Oh, I just thought it was amusing that you put in all this effort just to sleep.
We don't get this chance every day, so I'm gonna make the most of it and get as comfy as I can.
Want to try it yourself?
Me? No need. As long as I have my robe to wrap myself in, I can sleep anywhere.
I mean, sure, you could... But you could also get in this sleeping bag and close your eyes. It's sooo comfortable, you'll see.
Really?
Can't hurt to give it a try, can it?
Alright...
Hup...
Just squeeze yourself in there. I won't zip it up.
Mm, it's soft. Like beast skin... But it feels lighter than that...
Mmm...
These are our rations for tonight.
Alright, thanks. You can leave Typhon's over here.
What's wrong with her?
I had her try out my sleeping bag, and she fell asleep, just like that.
I need to step out for a second and speak with the shaman here.
If you want to go outside, you should take Typhon with you.
The forestfolk may be friendlier, but they won't appreciate it if you don't follow their rules.
Okay, got it.
Have a safe trip, Simone!
Hey... Typhon. Typhon?
She seems pretty exhausted.
I'll just take a few photos from the doorway...
Hmm...
(In Sami) You requested my presence, ma'am?
(In Sami) Yes, yes. Thank goodness you've come.
(In Sami) Allow me to explain the situation. As you can see, we are preparing to move.
(In Sami) We encountered some trouble on our journey from the mountains. Are you facing similar issues here?
(In Sami) Indeed, indeed.
(In Sami) Thankfully, there are no victims yet, but my tribesmen tell me of a number of ill omens we've received.
(In Sami) Most importantly, there's this.
(In Sami) 'Korona,' 'Hreidr.'
(In Sami) Samivilinn is clear. Regardless of the reason, my tribe must depart for a more suitable home.
(In Sami) I understand.
(In Sami) But then, why did you summon me...?
(In Sami) Please take a look at this.
(In Sami) I mustn't. This is your tribe's register.
(In Sami) I am an outsider.
(In Sami) I understand your concerns, my friend.
(In Sami) I've lived in this forest ever since I was a child. As did my father, his father, and all of our ancestors.
(In Sami) But because we have lived such quiet, comfortable lives for so long, this register has become barely more than a symbol for us, an inheritance.
(In Sami) When I flip it open, I find myself unable to understand all of its words.
(In Sami) I've studied it with my family, and communicated with my ancestors in hopes of understanding of the symbols within.
(In Sami) But I feel as though we are still missing something.
(In Sami) You are a shaman too, are you not?
(In Sami) I was.
(In Sami) As long as you were once, your wisdom will undoubtedly be of assistance to us, friend.
(In Sami) Please have a look. We need your advice.
Hah!
Sleeping bags are actually pretty comfortable...
Magallan!
Strange... Where is she?
Things seem pretty noisy outside...
Hm... 'Outlander...' 'touching the clan tree'?!
Oh no, it has to be that birdbrain!
Speak.
I'm sorry! I knew I couldn't take samples, but I had no idea I wasn't even supposed to touch it!
*sigh*...
The tribe's tree is their oldest, most respected presence. Under normal circumstances, an outsider wouldn't even get the chance to stay in their tree houses.
Would you touch an ancient monument for no good reason? That, or... Do you have any wise elders where you're from?
Hm... The President?
President, okay. If you were to run up to your president and touch their face, how would others react?
I'd probably be tackled to the ground by secret service agents before I got within ten feet of him...
It's like that, then.
Luckily, these people are peaceful. All they've done is talk behind your back. If we were anywhere more hotheaded, they might have taken an axe to your head.
I'm sorry...
Simone told you to take me with you when you go outside. How could you forget?
I just wanted to take a few pictures, but the tree looked like it had a nice texture, so I wanted to see it up close, and...
Wait, huh? You heard what she said? Weren't you asleep?
I could hear her while asleep.
Th-Then how come you didn't wake up when I called your name?
Because I was asleep.
Ugh...
Anyway, you've learned your lesson. Don't get hung up on it. Do you want to try some tasty fruits and nuts?
They have any here?
I saw a lot of them in the forest.
Come, we'll pick as many as we can. Enough for Simone too. We can eat them on the road tomorrow.
Mm-hmm.
You two are back?
Mmhmm!
We got some fresh fruits. Do you want any, Simone?
Mmmmmm?
We should rest now.
Mm.
Magallan.
Mmm?
Typhon, what did you feed her?
It wasn't me. She picked some nuts and asked if they were poisonous. I said no, and before I could even finish, she'd stuffed her mouth full of them.
Silencing chestnuts?
Mhm.
You should rest, then. A good night's sleep ought to fix it.
Hmm...
Good night.
Good night, Simone.
Mm mmmmm.
Mmmm!
What's wrong?
Mm.
You keep the sleeping bag. I don't need it.
Sweet dreams.
Magallan can still remember how fluffy and warm her sleeping bag was, and how rhythmic the breathing of those around her was.
It was a quiet, comfortable environment, just perfect for resting.
But the moment she opens her eyes, she finds herself in the forest.
A blanket of greenery drapes over her waist. The trees around her have shrouded the moonlight above. It is completely dark all around.
She feels thirsty, so she follows the sound of water to a stream. She bends down to drink from it.
Nothing is out of the ordinary. Everything is exactly as it should be, even when she sees the reflection in the water.
Even if the reflection in the water is that of—
A musbeast.
*Squeak*

The night is dark, quiet, and full of terrors. Magallan instinctively senses danger. She quickly lies down and breezes through the bushes before climbing onto the tree marked with her secretions, the nest she calls home.
*Squeak*!
It is safe there. There, she will find her kin, her family. She stands on the tree branch and squeals, waiting for a reply.
Nothing.
She hesitates a little, but in the end hops onto the tree trunk and back into her nest.
There, her mother is fast asleep in the shadows.
She wants to snuggle up to her, to get through the night in her embrace, but once her eyes adjust to the darkness, she finds only a scattered pile of beast bones.
The nest is very wide and very dark, almost like there's something waiting for her.
Waiting for her to come home.
Waiting for her to lower her guard.
Waiting for her to be overwhelmed by fear.
Something climbs onto the back of her neck—
Mm... ugh...
The nightmare left Magallan feeling sick. She wants to walk out the door, catch her breath, and calm herself down before going back to sleep.
But the moment she steps outside, what she sees makes her doubt that she has actually awoken from her dream.
There is a large, shadowy entity roaming here.
It looks like a person, and also kind of like a tree. The large shadow is moving in a single direction at a slow yet steady pace.
The entity shrouds even the moon above, keeping the ground below out of the moonlight's reach.
What... is this?
I need to get a picture of it.
*Click*—
Th-That's not possible. It makes no sense!
In the photo she just took, the shadow was nowhere to be seen and the moonlight illuminates the ground. Everything looks perfectly normal.
But the shadow stills moves before her eyes.
Without a sound.
Magallan stands rooted to the spot.
She doesn't know if she should head back inside to sleep as if nothing had happened...
...Or go grab her equipment and measure this possibly unrecordable supernatural phenomenon as best she can.
Just as she finds herself lost in thought, the enormous shadow sweeps past her. She instinctively jumps back, and at the same time—
Something climbs onto the back of her neck—
What are you doing out here?
Ah!
*sigh* Relax, Magallan. Calm down. I'm here. Nothing here is going to hurt you.
Huff... Haah... Phew...
Don't try talk yet. Calm down first.
Mmm...
Have a seat, and drink some water. It doesn't matter how big that guy is. It's just a shadow.
Huff... huff—
Feeling any better?
Mm, thanks. You almost scared me to death.
You're stronger than I expected. I was looking forward to you screaming loud enough to wake everyone up.
Are they going to kick me out of the village tonight, then?
No, no. The Sami aren't that narrow-minded.
...Had a nightmare?
Mhm.
Let me guess. One where a beastling cub goes home, finds its mother's bones, and then gets eaten?
Did you have the same dream?
Yep. Just this afternoon in your sleeping bag.
I'm sorry...
It's not your fault. It comes whenever I rest in a safe, relaxing environment.
Like an unwelcome friend.
This is one of the bad things about Sami. The cycle of hunt or be hunted never ends, even in your dreams.
And in dreams, most find themselves on the side of the hunted...
Then... can we become the hunters?
Wouldn't you rather be the rules themselves?
Is that even possible...?
Who knows?
Maybe it is.
Once we become more mature...
When we're no longer afraid of these big guys.
Magallan, why don't we try touching it?
Huh? Isn't that dangerous?
I thought you were an explorer? Why are you so afraid?
I only ever set out after I've planned for every possible contingency, but how am I supposed to prepare for this?!
S-Stop pulling—
Aghhh! We stepped on the tree!
It's not the main trunk. Stepping on it a little won't hurt.
Besides, no one's around to see.
Hup.
This is a great spot. Let's sit here.
A-Are you sure we're not too close?
How are we supposed to touch it from far away?
Are we... really going to touch it?
It's just a shadow. There's nothing to be scared of.
Or to exact, it's only a shadow for now. A very, very faint one.
Maybe one day it will grow into a more concrete threat, but we'll have grown too. We'll have even more tricks up our sleeves.
So when that time comes, maybe these things won't be so scary to us anymore.
Besides, I can sometimes hear a voice in that dream where we're preyed on.
'If something ever scares you, you should attack it without hesitation.'
'If you defeat it, you will have defeated fear. If you run from it, you will always be afraid.'
Come.
But...
Magallan uneasily looks at the curtain-like shadow before her.
The cruel and oppressive feeling she felt in her dream still haunts her mind.
Part of her tells her not to take the risk, but—
She slowly reaches her hand out.
Just like that. Slowly. It'll be fine.
I'll do it with you.
Typhon places her palm on the back of Magallan's hand and gently pushes it forward.
The moment they're met with a hint of resistance, she stops pushing forward, and instead reaches forward ahead of Magallan's hand.
She dips her hand into the shadow and pulls it back out. Then, she places it back on Magallan's hand.
See? Nothing to be afraid of.
Mm... I'll give it a try...
Magallan's hand inches forward bit by bit, until her fingertips are finally submerged in the shadow.
She thought she would feel a hint of cold, or perhaps that something would suck her in or chew on her fingers.
But there was nothing.
Nothing.
First, up to a joint. Then, a whole finger. Finally, her whole hand.
The shadow still moves, but aside from the change in the light, nothing happens at all.
Exactly as Typhon said.
It's just a shadow.
How is it? Just like I said, right?
You're right... There's nothing.
Hm... How did something like this happen in the first place...?
Still hung up on those scientific principles of yours?
Things don't just happen without rhyme or reason.
Mm-hm.
Then how come our mothers gave birth to you and me, and not to anybody else?
Based on your logic, isn't that something with no rhyme or reason?
W-Well, that's because...
It seems like you southerners really need to have a reason for everything.
But if you ask me, I think it's more important to be able to face the unknown at any time.
Even if I'm not prepared.
Don't you... get scared?
Do you? When you encounter a blizzard in the icefield, an avalanche in the mountains, or a pack of beasts when you're alone?
I do, yeah... but I still think I'll manage to survive somehow.
If you can manage to survive even when you're scared, you've already accomplished the most difficult thing there is.
And in that case, what else is there to be afraid of?
Even if the shadow in front of us were to suddenly turn into a real monster, you wouldn't be afraid, would you?
If it were something that actually exists, we could deal with it. This? This is just a shadow.
Everything that exists has a weak point.
You wait, observe, and survive until the moment you find that weakness, and then you attack. That's how hunters have always lived.
You're an explorer, so you're something like half a hunter. I'm sure you can handle these things, too.
Nothing stays the same forever. Maybe there will come a day when the things that once scared you are afraid of you instead.
Don't overthink it. We have to be on the road tomorrow.
Oh, right. Here.
Want a fruit before you go back to bed?
No thanks. Eating sugar before bed is how you get cavities.
Alright, up to you.
Good night.
Nighty-night, Typhon.
Magallan turns around and looks at the hovering shadow once more.
But for some reason, she doesn't think it represents fear and emptiness this time.
It feels more like a traveler who has lost his bearings and doesn't know where to go. Simply wandering the lands aimlessly.
Maybe, by the time the sun rises, it will have gone back home for a good night's sleep.
*Click*—
Magallan takes another picture. Just like before, there is nothing in the photograph.
She thinks a little, and adds a note on the picture.
'Mr. Lonesome.'
Finally, everything is quiet once more.
Only the wandering shadow is left to hover around alone.
Until daybreak.
It should almost be time.
I wonder what kind of ceremony this is.
Ooh, it's starting!
(Takes out her notebook) 'The Sami begin the ceremony by singing and dancing around their clan tree. Perhaps this is meant to be a farewell to the tree.'
Ten minutes later...
Hmm...
'The dance is very unique, but disorderly. Everyone dances on their own.'
'It's almost like a carnival.'
Thirty minutes later...
(Yawns)
Is Simone here yet?
Oh, she's right under the tree, talking to the shaman.
If you wanna look, I can lend you my binoculars.
No thanks. My eyes are enough to see her without your gadget.
What kind of ceremony is this, anyway? And how much longer will it take? If you hadn't come, I'd have fallen asleep by now.
I'm a Sami Sarkaz, not a Sami Elafia. I don't know much more than you.
As for how long it will take, that's hard to say. I've seen ceremonies that ended with just the shaman's nod, as well as gatherings that lasted all day and night.
But forget the ceremony. Take a look at this. I found it in the forest!
Is this... a snowball?
It's Amma's Affection, Magallan! This is Amma's Affection!
Amma? That means 'mother,' right?
Good memory.
As a southerner might put it, this is a mother's love.
It's the protection most sought after by the Sami. Among all of Sami's kindnesses, it's the purest and most sincere.
Here, take it Magallan. Put it in your pocket.
Didn't you just say all the Sami want this? You found it, so you should keep it. I can't take it.
I know its worth, but that's why I don't have the right to it.
Amma doesn't like granting protection to greedy people.
But you know nothing about her. Ignorance is also a kind of purity, and you have an honest soul. That's why you should have it.
Then... what should I do with it?
Forget about it, like you never received it in the first place.
Then I gotta give you something in return, too.
Hold on...
Here you go, Li'l Typhoon.
What did you just call me?
...Never mind. So what is this?
A flare. Just twist open the lid and pull the string, and it'll let out a ton of light and noise. You're a hunter; I'm sure can think of a use.
That does sound pretty useful. I'll try it out next time.
Huh? Magallan, they stopped dancing. Aren't you taking notes? Quick!
Huh? Oh! Okay!
(Pulls out notebook) 'After forty straight minutes of independent carnival, the ceremony has moved onto its next phase.'
'The shaman shows the tribesmen a woodcarving.'
'She says something impassioned.'
'Then, she raises her wooden staff, faces the clan tree, and sounds the horn. Then she starts murmuring something.'
'There is another shaman assisting her by her side, like she is singing an accompaniment.'
Typhon, can you help me out and figure out what they're saying?
Hmm...
I can't understand it.
The tone is familiar, but those are clearly not words you use in normal conversation.
(Jots notes) 'Some sort of ritualistic language.'
She's repeating a few short words over and over.
'Repeating...'
'Her emotions build up, and she keeps raising her voice—'
W-What's going on?
An earthquake? Typhon?! Where should we evacuate to?!
No... Magallan...
Look... The tree...
(In Sarkaz) My god.
Hmm...?
The tree's roots begin to emerge as it gradually lifts off the ground.
A thunderous sound rings through the forest and suppresses the shouts of everyone underneath.
Surrounded by other trees, it slowly rises into the air as its roots wring together into a bundle and form an unprecedented—in human terms—mobile structure.
Its leaves come falling down from the canopy, but the Sami are not shocked in the slightest. Instead, they appear very excited.
Indeed, what could be more encouraging than your tribe's ancestral tree lifting itself from the ground along with all the clan's history and memories?
(In Sami) Congratulations, Attendant. The ritual was a success.
(In Sami) I've always thought my ancestors' journey with the Tree was a mere tale.
(In Sami) Thank you, child of the Santalla Tree. Without your interpretation and assistance, this awakening ceremony would not have been successful.
(In Sami) The Tree has awakened, but It still awaits a direction and Its next steps from us. I must research our tribe register more.
(In Sami) With the Tree's enormous body out in the open, the disaster of the north is bound to notice it.
(In Sami) Their activity has been growing more and more frequent of late. You must pray for Sami to protect the Tree with you.
(In Sami) Yes. Yes, you are right.
(In Sami) Please make your way to the south with all haste and replant the Tree. Do not let yourselves get caught up in them.
(In Sami) Understood. Hm... The register says...
(In Sami) Well, then. My companions and I must take our leave. I pray that Sami will find you and your tribe a satisfactory destination, such that you need never relocate again.
(In Sami) Oh, are you leaving so soon?
(In Sami) You still have much to attend to. No need to see us off.
(In Sami) Then I wish you and your companions a safe and uneventful journey. Once again, I thank you kindly for your help, honored friend... Oh... I see... To make the Tree set out, I must first...
(In Sami) Farewell.
Report.
(Imitating the Ursus Messenger's voice) I have entered Sami as planned. Please advise on the positions of Liebafowl and the Black Mark. Over.
Hmph...
Simone, Simone!
Yeah, I'm back.
Is there something I can help you with?
It's time to go.
I'm sorry that took so long.
It's okay. Let's go.
Simone, you really need to explain to me what she said during the ritual. It's going to be real important to my research!
I'll tell you all about it on the way.
Sounds good.
What's the matter?
Nothing. I'll be done real quick!
*Click*—
Before she leaves, Magallan snaps a picture of the enormous tree standing in the forest.
For some reason, she feels that this gigantic being almost completely blocking the sun looks kind of familiar—
She doesn't think too much about it, and merely scribbles her immediate thoughts into her notebook.
Then, she follows the rest of the group and continue their journey to southern Sami.
Hiss—
Huff—
Hiss—
You can take off the breather, Magallan. There are no spores here.
Oh, okay.
Whew... hah...
I've never been so scared of mushrooms in my life.
I warned you, Maggy: Don't touch anything, don't touch anything, and don't touch anything.
Now you know why.
M-My—
The empty box you gave me? I put the mushrooms inside and sealed it. Here.
Th-Thanks.
*pant*—*pant*—
I didn't expect the spore density to be so high, or that it could paralyze the respiratory system.
It's a carnivorous mushroom. Where do you think its food comes from?
Ugh...
So how come you aren't affected at all?
We hunters have ways to deal with them.
If I died in one of those mushroom swamps, I'd never live it down.
(Sami) Typhon, the sacrifice.
(Sami) Sacrifice... are we there?
(Takes a deep breath)
You're right.
Where are you going? Wait for me!
And what do you by 'sacrifice'? Me?
Of course not, you birdbrain.
This is the heart of Sami, the First Forest.
Where we come from, and where we go.
Prepare some food as tribute to Sami, and we can enter.
Would bad things happen if we don't?
Who knows? Do you want to find out?
Er, never mind.
Is hardtack okay?
No idea.
Crush it and spread it on the ground.
A-Alright.
(Sami) Great land, we ask your leave to pass over you.
(Sami) We offer you this sacrifice, that you may watch over us, and protect us from bloodshed, from being hunted.
(Sami) Sami says it's okay, ladies.
(Sami) It will eat well thanks to your generosity.
Isn't that a little... casual? Young shaman.
...Apprentice shaman.
This isn't really a formal occasion, after all. Sami wouldn't mind the presence of a child of the Santalla Tree.
A shaman, a hunter, and an outlander? That's a rare sight.
I don't suppose you're here just for the experience?
We're in a hurry to head south, and don't have time to go around the First Land.
I see. My teacher often takes this shortcut too.
Not this time, though. He left me here alone to 'experience the Samivilinn.'
I've been here for days, wandering the woods without a sliver of revelation to be found.
You should know what I mean, child of the tree.
Please, call me Santalla.
Very well, Santalla. Can you tell me what our teachers expect us to bring back from here?
I do not know, oak-child. I have only been here to witness. This is not where my trial was.
Is that so? What a shame.
We have a long road ahead of us. Let's hurry.
Hmm...
Wait!
I'll come with you.
I know the trees with the best shade, the cleanest streams, and the safest animal trails.
I can be your guide!
There's no one more qualified!
Let me come with you, Santalla. I'm so bored here I could turn into a tree.
What do you think, great shaman?
And what about you, hunter? Or our outlander friend?
I guess I don't mind...
We're all agreed, then?
Let's go, then! Welcome to the First Forest!
Hmph...
One more question, oak-child.
Hm? What is it?
What should we call you?
Me? Hm...
My Sami name might be a little hard to remember... My teacher calls me Oakcup, so you can too.
May I ask how you got that name?
Because he always grabs my horn and tries to drink from it when he's drunk.
Oh...
Hey, are we being watched?
Like, by a deity...?
Big predators. One on the tree, one behind Simone in the bushes, and one behind that tree to the left.
Oh, is that all?
After all the weird stuff we've encountered, dealing with carnivorous wildlife seems almost quaint.
Should I be ready for a fight then?
No need. They're not hungry enough to risk attacking.
So stop worrying about their dinner and start worrying about ours—!
Huh? Look out!
Need help?
No thanks, I'm done.
A tuskbeast lies prone on the ground, glaring at Typhon.
A gaping wound stretches from its cheek all the way to its foreleg.
I guess one of them was hungry enough. Terrible choice of prey, though.
Go. Your meat tastes terrible anyway.
The tuskbeast does not seem willing to leave. It crouches down in the shadows, ready to attack again.
Bang!
A loud bang rings out in the air before it, sending it scampering into the distance and leaving behind only a scattered trail of blood.
Did you just shoot something with your finger, Typhon?
Yeah, for scaring away big guys like these.
A roll of leaves?
You wrap up air with leaves until it forms a ball.
(Takes notes)
But how do you wrap air with leaves?
Don't ask. I've tried to teach others, but no one's ever been able to learn it.
Easy, it's just an unlucky hornbeast that got its neck bitten in half. Maybe by the one that attacked us, maybe by another.
Hm.
Looks like our outlander friend has grown accustomed to the ways of this land.
A few more weeks of this, and she'll be a fully-fledged Sami.
Ahaha...
An outlander who understands Sami. How nice.
I remember working as a guide for some Columbian tourists while on a pilgrimage.
They started screaming when they saw some carnivores doing their carnivoring. Apparently, they thought it was too bloody and uncivilized.
Not that I'd call myself civilized, but didn't they pay good money to come here and see the 'untamed wilderness'?
One of them even tried to stop the beasts, and nearly ended up as their next meal.
Their clothes were dignified enough, but certainly not the people wearing them.
You have quite a mastery of Columbian.
(Ursine) Not just Columbian! I just can't keep my mouth shut. Guess I'm a natural-born chatterbox.
And I suppose my teacher was a weird old man for teaching me all that.
(Broken Sami) Know.. a bit... ah, yes, true.
The folks back home hate it when I just strike up a conversation with southerners. One time, there was a regular Ursus caravan that passed through the tribal market, and...
(When was the last time I returned?)
(The breath of the wild continues to echo through this place...)
(Does Amma still watch over this forest?)
(Something isn't right.)
(The influence of Amma lingers... but it's not alone.)
(The Black Mark of Ursus...)
What is it, Simone?
Nothing.
Has our guide found somewhere we can spend the night?
Yup, inside that tree up ahead.
Cup says it's hollow, with enough room for the four of us.
I've never seen a hollow tree before.
Then now's your chance.
Wow, we could easily fit another person or two in here!
It's the largest tree house around. Could hold close to a dozen.
Where's Typhon?
The hunter? She went looking for musbeast fruit stashes. She wanted something extra for dinner.
You should rest. I'll set up some protection.
Is that really necessary? The tree's already been blessed, and there are even talismans hanging off of it.
She's so serious.
That's how Simone is. I'd have frozen to death three, maybe four times by now if she weren't here to help.
Simone? Hm... Simone... Santalla... I think I've heard that name.
Is she someone famous in Sami?
Oh... No, not at all. I must be remembering wrong.
I have a hard time keeping track of anything I haven't seen for myself on my pilgrimage.
So maybe I'm mixing her up with someone else.
Mm.
(Opens notebook)
You seem to know a lot, Cup. Mind if I ask a few questions?
Shoot.
I picked up two pieces of bark from a tree. And they had symbols on them.
I want to know what they mean.
It's a code that Sami uses to communicate with us.
They could be messages about the past, the future, or about nature.
The same symbols can also mean very different things, depending on who is receiving them and when.
May I have a look?
I have them right here.
This is... the old tree's bark...
'Snowpriest'... 'Delight'...
Looks like a message from a clan tree to its shaman. You can have them back.
Mm-hm.
What's the difference between a 'Snowpriest' and 'shaman'?
Think of a Snowpriest as the head shaman or a greater shaman. He may not be the oldest, but he's certainly the highest in status, the most powerful, and the local leader.
Incidentally, my teacher is a Snowpriest.
Will... you become one too?
Nah, it's such a pain taking care of the tribe and solving all their problems. Leave that to someone who wants the job.
So... are you a shaman now?
Nope, just an apprentice. I have no idea what Sami wants, or even what it wants from me.
Which is why my teacher made me come here to feel the Samivilinn.
And have you felt it?
Nope.
Leaves fall, rivers flow. It's all the same. I don't feel anything unusual.
I have plenty of complaints for him the next time I see him.
You're back, Simone!
Mhm.
Let me have a look.
Wow. You can really do that with ground frost?
By the way, I heard that the one who serves the old tree can control the wind and snow, making them dance at her fingertips.
The old tree takes no servants, nor does it need one like me.
Well, if you don't want to talk, I'm not going to press the matter.
Old tree... Is there a difference between the clan trees?
There's only one tree that can be called old in Sami.
It represents the Past, and often appears with the heralds of the Present and the Future.
The three are separate but one, transcending Sami.
The Past...
By your outfit, I thought you were an explorer.
I am.
You seem more like—
What's the word?
A folklorist?
As an explorer, it's always good to learn more about an unfamiliar place.
I'll bring you some Sami books if we ever meet again, explorer.
That'd be great!
On the other hand, there's only so much I can carry in extreme environments... Maybe the equivalent of two volumes of the 'Encyclopedia Columbiana.'
It's about this long, this wide, and this thick... hardcover.
Yeah, not sure why you thought that'd help me at all. Just give me your luggage and let me feel the weight.
Oh, it's all bundled up to be carried on my back. One second.
There.
It's all on the ground.
Hm. Good, looks like you can fit it.
Huh?
That's about the same weight as two Sami books.
Oh.
Er... what sort of books?
Legends, tribal history, that sort of thing.
But a full tribal history takes up enough wooden tablets to fill an entire tree house, so I'll spare you that.
I-I thought the paper books I saw in the Cyclops's cave were Sami books...
Maggy!
Oh, welcome back.
Do you have any more of those flare sticks?
Yeah, why?
I tried one when I was picking the fruits, and it worked even better than the leaf ball.
You mean you ran into a tuskbeast?
Why wouldn't I? I was walking alone in the forest and they're hungry.
Anyway, do you have any?
Half a bundle. How many do you need?
Give me two for now.
And here, fruit from a musbeast stash. I've washed them with water.
Thanks!
Let's eat together and get to bed early. I don't want to wake up and find the door blocked by a bunch of hungry beasts.
I'll take the night watch.
You already stayed up the other night. It should be my turn.
It's safer here, and I won't be up all night. Consider it a chance for me to relax.
Alright... if you say so.
(Whisper) Be careful. There are demons in the forest.
I know.
Let's make a campfire, Simone.
Absolutely not!
Do you want to draw the attention of every predator in the forest?!
A-A lamp, then?
Not much difference between a lamp and a fire, light-wise.
If you can't sleep, then try this.
A dreamcatcher. Helps you pass the night in peace, with no dreams of becoming prey.
Thank you.
I'll be going to bed, then.
Good night.
As Oakcup promised, Magallan was not troubled by nightmares that night.
Instead, she dreamed another dream.
In it, she is still in the forest at night.
But she is not alone.
She is a creature like Emperor.
Beside her, a massive white hornbeast slumbers in the dream of another.
It was serene and beautiful. Magallan could not help but reach out to caress It.
But It is so dignified and solemn that her hand stops in mid-air.
She decides to lay down next to It and dream together with It.
A dream within a dream.
In it, a dark shadow shambles towards the tree hole where the group is sleeping.
Behind it is a trail of darkness and filth.
Black snow falls from the sky, burning away Santalla's frost and creating a gap.
The shadow walks towards the gap, leaving its vile trail on the ground.
Suddenly, a hornbeast catches its attention.
The hornbeast leaps and disappears. By the time the shadow turns around, the gap is filled with frost again.
It gazes upon the frost, and shambles towards where the hornbeast disappeared.
The hornbeast is still by Magallan when she wakes up.
But Its horn had begun to melt.
Then Its body, then the forest.
Magallan sits upon the snow, snowflakes falling on her beak.
For some reason, she feels regret.
She never sees the hornbeast's eyes, until the dream vanishes.
Oh!
You're up early today.
I just happened to wake up...
Perhaps that was not the entire story. Her sleeping bag is wet near the pocket.
She remembers pulling the pocket's zipper shut before sleeping, but it is open now, as if something fell out.
There was nothing missing though—
Except a little Sami souvenir.
Typhon...
What's the matter? Got out the wrong side of bed?
Does Amma's Affection disappear?
Mother has to take care of so many. She can't stay by your side forever.
Did the Affection I gave you melt?
Yeah...
Maybe that's a good thing.
Maybe she took care of a big problem for us, without us even realizing.
If possible, I'd have liked to keep it with me.
Well, we can always find another. It's not that hard.
Oh, okay!
That's... the beast that attacked us the other day.
Hasn't been dead for long.
